And then you've got this very lush, incredibly well-funded—nobody, each mega corp can't outdo each other for investing more in just doing more. So you've got chips absolutely running—SK Hynix in South Korea, flying, Samsung, you name it. That index is also spat up even further than anyone could imagine. And the AI's NASDAQ all running up.

And that's literally the last bull, bull that—that had a sort of a short squeeze element to it. It's gone up so violently after the correction. It's even gone up in gold ounces during this period of gold correction. But when you look at it on the big time frame—and it's probably worth going there again, even on the IXIC, and showing everybody—that it is a violent rally in a bear market. Some of the most violent rallies occur in bear markets.

纳斯达克 vs 黄金：1999 与今天

So if I just go and get the NASDAQ up on a nice juicy time frame, you can see how strongly it's gone up just here on that. It's a real violent—almost a short squeeze. But let's just get the gold back on that XAU. Let's go gold rather than silver. And because my thesis is, all terms of valuations, the only thing that's reliable nowadays and is fixed is a gold ounce.

And that was—that's the only part also that I would say you haven't run. This was a great time to be in tech stocks till '99. Then actually you needed for an in a full decade—11 years—you should have been in metals for that period. Then you got a rebound rally from around 2011 to 2021. You got another decade where it was great to be in tech stocks. You're not getting to the highs, but this was tech winning.

It's quite easy to see now structurally if you have a look at that and you do the technical analysis on that—that is a reversal. And by the way, you got a reversal in both the dot-com reversal, and the target was made very clearly. There was that this was an extreme blowoff. You can see how violent, how sharp the edges were. That was an excellent bottom corner. And now you're in a more rounded—a slightly more rounded, that's been a bit more nuanced, but still a head and shoulders version of that, because they've created this AI bubble again.

And you are in a rally period, but that's your shoulder. And when does—when are we wrong? You know, when does it—ah, you're wrong, the tech market was the better place to be than gold. You need to get above 6.57. We always have a level where we accept we're wrong. If you don't, you're going to get cleaned out.

But our opinion is this is a rally in a triggered head and shoulder. There's the armpit one and armpit two. There's your left shoulder. There's your head. There's your soft right shoulder. Was a bit of a small one. And there was complexity in the left shoulder, by the way. And you'd often tend to get the same here. So it wouldn't be a shock for us to have this happen a bit before that happens, because you had it over here.

So this is a rally and some of the most violent rallies can happen in a bear market. But you were at 8.97 ounces to buy the NASDAQ market in 2021/22. Now you're at 5.5. Now it's a heck of a fall. You've lost 40%. The tech stock market has lost 40% to gold between, and that's a fact. These are the numbers.

It'll shock people sometimes when you say this: "No ways, they haven't, we're in a meltdown." Here's where you were mid-2021. Whatever the date is there. If you really want an exact date, I'll give it to you. You needed 9 oz of gold—that's the top, the very peak, at the very top. You needed 9 ounces of gold to buy a weighted basket of the NASDAQ. Today, even on top of all the rallies that are happening, you're at 5.6. 5.6 versus 9. You do the maths on that.

The best place to have been has been gold. Is gold winning right this week, this particular month? No. You've had three months down. But look at all the months you've been winning. That's your curve of performance so far, and it's pretty strong in your favor for holding gold. And this pattern suggests to me it will get stronger. You'll be pointing a steeper line deeper. Just give it more time from here.

And don't forget that's without counting a major tech crash. I think there's more fluff in the tech market than there's in the gold. Even if people are forced to sell some gold because of margin calls, tech market will fall a lot for—it'll have a much higher beta. That thing can lose 40%, 50%, it could even lose 60%. You know, Amazon went from $106 back down to $6. Gold's not doing that. Gold isn't doing that. Even if people have to sell, gold won't be doing that.

白银 $330：HVF 目标与不止于此

So, sorry. To put this in perspective also, because now you've had like the gold to stocks ratio. If we now look at where silver is headed, because you've just mentioned that the bottom is drawn at $75 an ounce more or less. And I remember six months ago you said, well, in a blowoff top it could even reach above $333 an ounce. So, are we still on track for this heavy move up, if everything stays as you analyze it?

Very much. And by the way, that's not our top call for silver. It's just our highest dollar price that we've gone public with so far. And it stems from a macro setup. I'll share the screen in a second. I'll just get this ready.

But the setup—and there's quite a bit to just say that this pullback that we've experienced was bang on time and where we expected it to be, in and around the $953 point—we were expecting a major pullback. And I will show that to you the minute I get the chart up. So I just get that drawn up quickly and show that to you.

And say that if we are done now with this pullback—I don't think we just go straight, who knows, we can go straight to new highs, trading higher just walk out. But I have a feeling we might sell off again at around before the last high.

第二中继目标

But this setup which we showed to you prior—our second interim being made was always saying that there's a major rest to come in around this point. So we've appeared on your show before at the 25, the 35, the 45, the 55 range, and we said your big rest will come when you make this dash line. We're still—it's amazing that we're doing this interview and the gold-silver ratio is still falling and that 15-minute silver chart is still running.

And we said you'll have a rest and a strong pullback when you run the second interim, here, and that was from the 90s. We had to trim this high point, which actually generates that. So it's more like the mid-upper 90s, because the high here that was actually in the 50s isn't registered correctly. So we had to do a minor manipulation on the chart.

But this is the 330 range. And once you've finished dealing with and you've had a pullback with the second interim and you get back above and you dealt with it, you wind up and then suddenly you go—this open territory—when you make the next run usually comes fast and furious. Then you have a pullback and you churn again. So this period here and here is going to be exceedingly good.

单位数金银比？

And that's for now our highest dollar forecast. But we've actually said single-digit gold-silver ratio, and we've got numbers, but because I don't want to go straight into the Max Keiser camp, we've held back on those. But I've got point-and-figure targets quite beyond that.

But I'd imagine you would have a period of rest, much like we've having now at the second interim, only maybe even a bit longer—but I wouldn't say too long—at the 330s level that we forecast. And HVF method is the most reliable targeting mechanism I have, and it consistently performs. It consistently provides interim levels.

And you can see how you got pushed back in the big time frames when you're looking at a quarterly chart. This whole pullback period could come down to nothing more than that one shooting star green candle that later gets rebuffed and trades all the way up. All the way up. If we do the walk out, we go all the way up. We run well through 333. We pause for a while, and then we go further again.

So because I'm utilizing leverage, I really am interested in the periods that are going to be quick, dominant, and are in between the interim and the final targets. And if we are finishing here, this could be a fabulous run.

So gold and silver people, you're in the right place. Don't go anywhere else. This is your beauty queen. Your NASDAQ is actually underperforming you since 2021 and typically you have a decade.

And by the way, whilst we've been talking—having just got off a community update putting my people long silver on a small time frame, a very small time setup which you can see here—this is what we do, by the way. During that period the gold and silver ratio hasn't stopped beeping and the silver price hasn't stopped beeping and this hasn't stopped moving up. So if you want a little bit of—this is what we do. Sorry, all the alerts were on. Sorry they were beeping throughout while we were interviewing.

But this is what we do. We really trade. We have the platforms on and we have put on leverage longs and we've made, whilst we're speaking, probably two to $2.5 from our entries. So it's been a great call. I'm very happy to see your handsome face, Alexi. It literally has been a great call in any sense of the term.

黄金图表与铜的"假牛"

So what about now the gold chart, if we—or maybe if you've shown that already before. And then after that I would be curious to have your perspective on copper. I haven't heard you say much lately about it, but I've also seen quite a few analysis coming by, and as we talk about the huge debasement, commodities will be thriving and copper is used in pretty much anything that is considered like the global economy, if it's about plumbing or even AI, electronics, all kinds of things. And also I would like, if we have time, to have a bit of your stance on uranium. I'm not sure if there's any of those that tickle your interest at the moment.

Everything tickles my interest that I can draw a chart, but I can tell you I've just shown you the best markets of them all being silver.

黄金：极向上突破

And this is a continuation chart. There's your pole. There's your blowoff. And that is a broad structure.

See it on gold. Am I sharing screen? Yeah. Let me just make sure I share the screen so everyone can enjoy that, or use a bit of imagination.

Yeah, yeah. Visualize this, friends. That is what I was highlighting. So, you know, that's your pole. That's your blowoff period for gold. This is a broadening structure. You had a little bounce at the splitter. You came and you bounced again. This is a hammer and you're going up. That's my take on gold. This is on quite a big time frame, daily.

I say it's not really big, but in terms of—because we are showing you things on a 15-minute, let me just take you down and show you that you had a gap open. I love that when that happens, and you turn around and you absolutely fly back up. That's what's happened on the silver, and I think the gold's going to follow. There's your opening gap on a Sunday. They try to push it in the way it's not going and it's rejecting and it's going to close that gap. And that was a flush out and you're going higher. That's my take from the Sunday open.

It's on a low time frame. You know, don't day trade this opinion. By the time you see it, it'll be delayed. We have real stops. We have money management. We trade for ours. We do a lot of things to maximize our wealth building. But that goes up.

铜：以金计价仍在下跌

You mentioned copper and uranium. I'll just do copper. Here's the thing that you should bear in mind. In fact, let's go futures and get ourselves the continuous chart. High grade. There it is. That's the one I want. And go to a big time frame.

So these things are going up. Remember this. They are showing you the debasement of fiat. Which one goes up more? Don't try me. Here's the tendency. When people see something go up, they want to be in all of them. I was in that. It's like you're winning many times by being in copper, by being in uranium, by being in silver, gold, platinum, zinc—you name them all.

And the key thing is you're going to make the most money by being in the best one. And I've shown you the best one. Because if I take this copper chart, which looks very positive and will go higher, it's because of the fiat debt debasements. It's going higher. It will go up generally even against many other bases of comparison.

But let's just use our gold matrix: 1 oz of gold. You divide it by 1 oz of gold and you go up. And it is in a massive falling trend in gold ounces. It's currently up. It's going to have a little catch-up because gold was currently down. Maybe you want to draw a falling wedge on that if you can. But that is not a small time frame chart that goes down to that.

And everybody wants to sound so innovative. "There's too many people talking about gold and silver. I'll be the copper guy. I'm going to keep talking about copper." You're underperforming gold and you have been from 1999 to today.

And the answer is quite simple. One is a monetary—a big-time monetary preservation of asset dense value—and the other one only in China to a degree has some monetary element, but otherwise is considered an industrial metal. Yes, it's important if you want to build all the green—it's all the stories you hear, it's all true, we don't have enough of it, I'm bullish it. But the point is the Porsche Turbo keys are there. Why are you jumping into the VW Polo? The one's going to smash it.

And everybody wants to be super clever. I like to make it simple. I get it all the time: "What about zinc? What about this? What about that?" They'll all go up and you can look 10 times smart because you're in 10 things, or you can just make more money. It's not about being right and how many times you get to be right. Make the money.

我们错过又抓回的金银

I start off often with the wrong view, and I have a very quick—you saw that on the gold-silver ratio. We admit straight up we were saying there's a high risk stock markets are peaky. This could break up. Maybe we'll get one more sell. We like the silver and gold charts. We want to be in. We wait for the gold and silver to confirm it could be up. Boom. It starts breaking down. Great. Best news. We're not panicking. And we charge in and we buy. We charge in and we buy. And that's what we've been doing.

Charging in and buying. And now we've been buying continuation patterns on 15 minutes and making money on that in silver. And I will close part of that position at the target and leave the rest on for the far bigger targets. So I've smashed in some bigger size with conservative leverage, with a stop close to its entry, that I can hold all the way to 333 and maybe beyond on our gold-silver ratio exits and some of the bigger numbers I've yet to mention publicly that we have on point-and-figure targeting.

And this is copper. It's underperforming. Yeah, for the quarter it's up. Well, you know what happened to gold during that quarter? Wait till gold starts going again. They'll both be going up, but now gold will be going up again, and this will stay growing along, grinding along the bottom. This is a bottom feeder against gold. It's an absolute bottom feeder.

Is it short? Is it useful? How much production is going to be going on? How much building's going to be going on if we have this debt-base crash? Are you going to build an extension? You going to buy your neighbor's house and knock his house down and extend and build, Alex, the Pink Gin Palace with your underground basement and your 14 cars that you also plan to be buying that will all be consuming copper? No. The jobs will be less. Your clients will have less money. They'll be spending less. You'll be doing well to still be in work. It will be CV-19 all over again. They have the power to rug the entire economy.

And this is an industrial metal. You want a monetary metal in a monetary collapse. If it's a fire, you want to have an asbestos suit and a fire hose. Other things can be useful, but not as useful as that. You know, that's where we are.

铀：好，但还不够

Again, let's do uranium. I like uranium a lot, but it still hasn't done what the monetary metals do of the energies. It's my favorite—uranium. I've actually—there's this recent proxy that I've discovered of FXCM, and it has a little bit of SPR stuff. These are the tickers in this—they call it uranium index if you want—CCJ, DNN. Some of you will know these. It hasn't been around for so long. This is a great chart. This is a great chart too.

But you know, if you look at how it's come since 2023, you divide it by—let's go gold, same old unit of account, your new unit of account, during periods when all fiats are being debased. You see how the chart—not so, it's not bad, but it's not as good. If you got in here, you've had a surge on uranium, but if you were here, you're down. You got in in 2024, you're down against gold. And if you want me to put silver on that, you're really down.

There's our silver streaker, by the way. Absolutely mooning. We're making big profits right here, right now. This is your champion.

So this is the like right—right now is just the perfect timing, you would say, like the fact that we have a call now. This is just confirming everything.

You know, you asked me. I generally only do interviews now on Wednesday and only once a week to keep my media appearances down and also to allow me time for other things. Because it's you, and I know you, I squeezed you in on Monday and here you are. The time you scheduled at 2:00 South African time, the setup was ready and that's what's happening.

That doesn't mean uranium is garbage. It's actually a very good—if you weren't holding gold and silver, I'd probably say uranium's maybe the next to have. But it's not quite the same. And again, as I say, I'm using one proxy—people have a lot of individual ones.

货币金属、白金与稀缺溢价

But gold and silver are the monetary metals. Silver is the greatest. It's going to be the biggest outperformer second half of this boom. I think platinum will become unobtainium and will probably outperform but on incredibly low volume. So it's the kind of thing you want to accumulate now on the basis you won't easily be able to get it at all. It'll probably become illegal unless you have a license.

So I think it's a worthy other if you're tired of stacking silver and you get more value in a denser smaller space if you've started to have storage problems. That's it. But everything else doesn't look as good.

数字游戏：铀 vs 白银的相对绩效

I mean, do you have a—what's your favorite proxy for uranium? The Global X Uranium ETF?

Sure, we can work with that. I mean, I don't have any particular one that I'm favoring.

So if we go up to monthly, let's just measure. So we kind of had a moment here in April when the market sold off—a relative low. Just show you by measurements what silver and this uranium proxy did. It's good. It just isn't first.

Give me that measurement tool. I trust we're still sharing screen right now as you stand. 182% was made from the 1st of April low. I like that. It's April Fool's Day. You're up into this continuation. 182%. No one's going to tell you that's a bad performance. No one in—that's 25. So it's about a year and one month because we kind of—that's, we're kind of 11 days into May. Yeah.

Let's get silver. Why I keep saying you're looking at Jelle and you're saying what about other girls? And here's silver. Even with a substantial correction baked in. There's the April low. Where are we now? Very close. Actually, a little bit. I was expecting it to be more 190, but don't forget that's after a 50% correction. You had a 47% correction, you had a rally, and then you had another 32% correction. Uranium didn't have that.

So the fact that silver with that level of correction is still ahead is surprising. Take it from July. This localized low for silver because I chose the best low for uranium. You're at about 383. It's taken away my proxy again. Let's just go—that's minus Global. There we go. URA. So when was it? July. July the year before: 175. Not the same. Not the same. Show you again on silver. Got to get my year right, sorry, I took my goggles off and now I'm getting a bit gagger. 287. It's significantly more. And that's after a 47% correction, a rally, and then another 40% correction.

So silver is the winner. Silver is the winner. You can invest in the others—commodity funds should do well. I'd just rather be in the best, the very, very best. Take the keys to the Porsche Turbo. All the others are not as fast.

白银驱动力：货币 + 工业的双重共振

No. And so now, so you just described it as now the consolidation phase being over. We've had the correction and now it's back up again. The rocket silver is igniting proportion. What are the let's say biggest factors and drivers that keep being the same for that growth?

What are the key drivers that will keep silver being up, you mean?

Yes. Because you just mentioned that a part of maybe China where the monetary aspect of silver is, or even India actually is important. It still has an industrial base most of all.

And that industrial base is all part of the agendas they're most trying to push: data centers, batteries, solid-state batteries, solar, all forms of energy that take you out of oil burning that give money to the Middle East. The Indians are trying to spend less on oil and on gold because it's killing the rupee.

印度悖论：莫迪让大家别买黄金

So here's an amazing simple summary—and I did this on X as well, and I also just highlighted this again with regards to the drivers on silver and also what it does to your fiat.

So the simple summary on this Indian crisis—and we'll come back to those drivers again—is: the more a nation believes in precious metals like gold and silver, which the Indians do, the more the phony local fiat (which is the rupee in that case) is compromised, exposed and quickly devalues.

So the more your indigenous population are net accumulators—why? Well, for coin number two: if you are importing most of that gold and energy, which India is doing as per that diagram that this gentleman posted, you are giving up your dollars which are your foreign reserves. As a rupee Indian nation, you're importing both gold and energy that you're paying for in dollars (but more in other currencies as well). Your foreign reserves are dropping chronically, and you lack dollars and other currencies like rubles and yuan if you're doing gold-backed yuan.

And you have a massive consumer population of both items, both gold and oil, because they drive, they catch taxis, their trains are driven, whatever the case may be with different forms of power that you have to import. You have a very weak balance of payments position. You run deficits. You don't own the fiats.

That's why he's tried to say, "Stop buying precious metals, you're killing our currency." And my suspicion is that was a very high-risk strategy. The smart Indians will go, "We're going to have a currency crisis, buy gold and silver" if they're smart.

白银的双重需求

And the drivers are all the industries—solar panels, all energy-related, the whole carbon footprint—all reliant on silver. Data centers, even military-industrial complex if you want to throw missiles around—all reliant on silver. So the demand isn't going away.

And China has decided they're going big and they're going to use the discount window that was afforded them. Of the second interim on the technical upside HVF, and we are holding for 333 and far greater wealth. And enjoying our money and recognizing that this is a fiat and debt-based collapse, and you need to build wealth, protect that wealth, and broaden your footprint out of the West. That's our key message.

2027 展望：全球能源锁定与恐惧产业

A late day.

On this note, Francis, I'm not sure if you want to share maybe your outlook on 2027. You've been saying this before we recorded. I'm not sure if you wanted to maybe share your outlook on the coming global energy lockdown that you forecast that will happen in 2027. You want to say a few words about that?

I'm feeling that the 2027—and I think part of it already starts during this year and can escalate in the second half of this year. It's not a day, it's a process of tightening. People are already commenting that this new virus has received untold amount of journalistic coverage and articles and everything that essentially owns two. They've already got Tedros out peddling his narratives and selling fear.

You got to remember that the main purveyors of fear that they sell—their product is fear and entrapment. So in the name of your safety, they strip you of your standard of living fully. This is what they do. This is what they do.

拒绝恐惧

And you—to not live in fear. Look at me in the eyes. I'm not afraid of whatever virus. I am absolutely, absolutely not afraid. Take it from me. You have an amazing immune system. Try to do less that compromises it, like industrial pharmaceutical medical interventions direct into your bloodstream. Remain healthy, enjoy life, live your life to your highest. Reject their fear. Switch off that MSM garbage. Build wealth. Restore your wealth. Enjoy your wealth. And help your friends. Build a community and get a broad footprint.

This is the key thing. And the escalation of these various agendas are already back on, and the surveillance grid is back on. And, you know, maybe we should be talking to each other on a ham radio like good old buddy, you know, Smokey and the Bandit style—citizen band radio CB and ham radio. Get your ham radio license from home, throwing these devices away, throwing these 24/7 breaking news that peddles fear and compliance and has you also zombified. Go live life, be outdoors, be in nature, ride motorcycles, be badass, and stop being afraid to exist, and stop giving power to these draconian overreaching psychopaths.

辛辛纳图斯的教训

I mean, I read a small, very small passage—and I'll give you just the punchline because we've been going for an hour now—but it was about Cincinnatus, a Roman. And the key story was, he was quite poor and he was on his farm. He was 60 years old and he was working his farm. He was an ex-senator. He resigned. He'd lost quite a lot of his money bailing out his son who got bankrupted. But he always gave up the power when he had it after he had done his job.

And the Romans were trapped. They needed an army raised. And they sent a small group to him and said, "You have full power. There's a decree. You are now the dictator of Rome. We are trapped. We need you." He put on his toga. He raised an army. He went and led the attack and got the ruling Senate that had been pinned in a valley released within 12 days. And he stood down within 21 days again, rejecting carrying on power, went back to his farm.

And believe it or not, the story goes he was called upon again and given absolute power again, as an 80-year-old, when there was an internal conspiracy that needed weeding out.

And the key point is: give power to those that almost don't want it and are quite happy to serve their citizens to do a job and then to walk away. And the problem we have is that we have a cycle of giving power to those that have never done any value-add, are psychopathic about retaining it, holding it and entrenching it at the cost of all the citizenry, and are not interested in service towards the greater good at all. And they're all compromised and they're all blacklisted.

And, you know, there's a city in America called Cincinnati after that Roman ideologue. And we could do a lot more if we gave more value and power to true servants that did their job of service for the very tight remit that they afforded themselves and couldn't wait to go home to the farm—the call-it-the-farmer, man-of-the-soil spirit of agreement that lives a simple life and works for others, and doesn't seek to hold power.

And this is our grand problem. We've got long-standing public servants like Tony Blair and Klaus Schwab and all of them that are all compromised, blackmailed and entrenched on giving a two-tier society where we are slave labor and can be turned off at any time and they are a superior ruling class. It's a supremacism. We face supremacism, and it isn't white supremacism. It's a supremacism of politics and culture that has captured us and runs a blackmail system against us and sees us all as expendable, like you would stand on your own ant farm and they have no sentiment or feeling.

结语：反抗与生命

And you're not going to live and go meet your maker and say, "I cowered in fear, paid all my taxes to pedophiles, blackmailed pedophiles who were nefarious predators and actively acted against me"—then you have lived the life of cowardice. And it's time to snap out of it and be a man. Be the woman you're meant to be. Go live the life of opportunity, the gift that it is, and push back against these forces. Call them to task. Resist everything they do and organize. Make their life a misery. Be determined to live a long healthy life and be a perpetual thorn in the side of the supremacist ruling classes that feel that they are superior to you and have the power to take your freedoms away from you.

And that's my overruling message. That's my doctrine and philosophy. And if that works, please find the Market Sniper channel on YouTube. You saw our community making money while we were talking. We'd love to do the same for you. And I hope that was fun and enjoyable, and it was great seeing you again.

And I look forward to seeing you tying the knot next year, providing we're not in some lockdown or engineered macro financial situation that prevents us from getting together. Actually, I'll see you soon in a few weeks time if we see each other in Cyprus.

Francis, as always, what an ending. Damn. I'll just look forward to already the comment section firing up about the exact segment. Beautiful, eloquently resumed. Thanks a lot for your time, your precious knowledge, and your exuberant energy.

Thanks for having me. Have a great weekend, guys. Yes.

item.description:
## 一、"特朗普创伤三角"的真正秘密：它不是三角，而是一个正在塌缩的相位空间
Francis 把油价、债券收益率、金银三者画成一个三角，但最颠覆性的洞见其实藏在他没明说的地方——**这个三角的边长正在缩短**。这不是普通的市场波动，而是热力学意义上的**相空间坍缩**：系统的自由度在减少。
过去油价能跑到 147 美元、利率能在 5% 待一段时间、黄金可以横盘——三者互不干涉。现在三者只要任何一个突破极小的阈值（油 115、十年期 4.5、黄金创新高），另两个就立刻反向爆破。这与生态学中"承载力崩溃前的临界减速（critical slowing down）"是同一种数学结构——**当一个复杂系统接近相变，其响应时间变长、波动幅度变大、彼此相关性骤升**。债券市场和大堡礁白化、亚马逊雨林干旱的预警信号，遵循着同一组微分方程。
换言之，Francis 在用技术分析的语言，无意中描绘了一个**生态系统级的临界现象**。
## 二、那个让人意外的观察：美国正在用"卖金子"伪装贸易顺差
这是整段对话里最被低估的炸弹——Francis 一句带过：**美国过去五个月之所以没有创下贸易逆差
